characters.physical_details: Average-height Hufflepuff boy with brown hair, an easily overlooked face, and robes he forgets to straighten. After the Chapter 6 Cruciatus, he develops an intermittent left-hand tremor. From Chapter 11 onward, First Art work causes recurrent nosebleeds, headaches, blurred vision, and afterimage-like visual distortion.

key_relationships.miren_gale: Best friend since first year; the friendship breaks when Gavin keeps choosing ward work over human-scale crisis.
key_relationships.duncan_flett: Housemate and friend whose suffering Gavin repeatedly notices without helping enough.
key_relationships.professor_babbling: Ancient Runes teacher who recognizes the manuscript as unprecedented, gives him Wexworth, then refuses further involvement.
key_relationships.amycus_carrow: Regime enforcer who repeatedly brutalizes him and other students.

characters.occupation_status: Fifth-year Hufflepuff in his OWL year. Ancient Runes elective. Middling student in ordinary wand magic at the start; by spring his grades and wandwork are collapsing because nearly all spare time goes to the manuscript and substrate literacy increasingly interferes with normal casting.
characters.literacy_languages: English only in ordinary language. By mid-novel he can read the Builders' notation, which is a perceptual/cognitive system rather than a spoken language.

state_transitions.change: Carrow detention leaves bruises, skinned knees, and stinging-hex burns.
First direct perception of substrate beneath ordinary magic.
Cruciatus exposure leaves an intermittent tremor in his left hand.
Sleep loss and substrate literacy begin interfering with ordinary wand casting, especially Transfiguration and nonverbal Charms.
First successful deliberate First Art edit; nosebleeds and visual strain become a regular cost.
Ordinary enchantments become legible to him without translating through the manuscript first.
First Art use starts happening reflexively, including an aborted substrate correction on a first-year's broken wrist.
Chooses continued ward work over intervening in the Battle of Hogwarts.

characters.availability: POV character and physically present in every chapter.

characters.physical_details: Tall, quick-moving, physically confident, with dark hair usually worn in a practical plait. Mixed heritage: Black British Muggle mother, white wizard father. Understands Yoruba but answers in English. By Chapter 12 she has an unexplained bruise on her jaw from resistance work; during the battle she adds a forehead cut and a burn on her left forearm.

key_relationships.gavin_tench: Best friend turned estranged witness to what the manuscript is making him.
key_relationships.duncan_flett: Housemate she actively protects through food, practical care, logistics, and attention.
key_relationships.alecto_carrow: Enemy through the Muggle Studies classroom and the anti-Muggle propaganda Miren keeps correcting in the margins.

characters.occupation_status: Fifth-year Hufflepuff. Better than Gavin at practical magic. Active in student resistance logistics: messages, healing supplies, lookout work, and support for hidden students.
characters.literacy_languages: English; passive Yoruba comprehension from home.

state_transitions.change: Confronts Gavin over his dissociation and manuscript obsession; friendship begins to fracture.
Resistance work becomes physically costly; jaw bruise appears and she is visibly carrying DA logistics.
Friendship with Gavin breaks after he refuses to step back from the manuscript.
Takes battle injuries while fighting and looking for Gavin.
Accepts that Gavin's cosmic claims are true even though she cannot share his frame of reference.

characters.availability: Strong presence in early-term friendship scenes, resistance scenes, the Chapter 14 break, the battle, and the aftermath.

characters.physical_details: Thin, fair-haired, light-skinned, and increasingly underweight across the year. By winter he looks older than fifteen. He often hides shaking hands inside sleeves or pockets. After Chapter 12 he carries a badly healed split lip and a more permanent wariness in his posture.

key_relationships.gavin_tench: Housemate and friend who sees Gavin drifting away long before Gavin notices Duncan disappearing into survival mode.
key_relationships.miren_gale: Primary source of day-to-day protection, food, and practical care.
key_relationships.alecto_carrow: Public target in Muggle Studies as a Muggle-born student.

characters.occupation_status: Fifth-year Hufflepuff, Muggle-born, from Sheffield with two Muggle parents. Formerly a good student; by spring he is mostly surviving school and avoiding danger to home.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Strong Muggle cultural knowledge, usually suppressed for safety.

state_transitions.change: Muggle Studies pressure becomes visible through forearm bruises and more careful self-erasure.
Marked weight loss, hidden hand tremor, withdrawal from common-room life, and open worry that going home could endanger his parents.
Cruciated in class as Amycus Carrow's demonstration; lip split and posture permanently altered.
Survives the battle after sheltering with younger students in the Room of Requirement.
Helps carry water in the Great Hall triage aftermath.

characters.availability: Visible in meals, classes, and late-night common-room scenes early on; increasingly withdrawn by spring; back in the aftermath.

characters.physical_details: Squat, lopsided, bad-toothed, and physically graceless, usually with robe sleeves shoved up. He sweats when casting Unforgivables or when his authority is under stress.

key_relationships.alecto_carrow: Sister and closest collaborator inside the regime.
key_relationships.severus_snape: Nominal superior whose authority he resents and tries to perform hardness for.
key_relationships.gavin_tench: Student he punishes early and later treats as one more body to discipline.
key_relationships.duncan_flett: Muggle-born student he uses as a classroom demonstration target.

characters.occupation_status: Mandatory Dark Arts professor, detention master, and Death Eater enforcer inside Hogwarts.
characters.literacy_languages: English; functionally an enforcer rather than a scholar.

state_transitions.change: Runs the mass Great Hall Cruciatus lesson and permanently injures Gavin's hand.
Uses Duncan as a Cruciatus demonstration in Dark Arts class.
Student resistance, disappearances to the Room of Requirement, and open corridor defiance begin stripping away his authority.
After Voldemort falls, the dark-magic structure supporting his side collapses with the rest of the battle.

characters.availability: Recurring threat through detentions, corridor patrols, Dark Arts class, and the battle night.

characters.physical_details: Heavyset and hard-faced like Amycus, but steadier and less theatrically unstable. Frequently wears dark-stoned rings and carries herself like someone who expects compliance.

key_relationships.severus_snape: Nominal superior under the Hogwarts occupation structure.
key_relationships.duncan_flett: Uses him and other Muggle-born students as public examples in propaganda lessons.
key_relationships.miren_gale: Faces direct resistance from Miren's fact-corrections and DA-linked student work.

characters.occupation_status: Mandatory Muggle Studies professor under the regime; teaches blood-purity propaganda rather than genuine Muggle Studies.

state_transitions.change: Installed as compulsory Muggle Studies teacher under Snape's headmastership.
Publicly humiliates Duncan in class and makes anti-Muggle propaganda a daily fact of school life.
Her classroom and authority are openly disrupted by student resistance.

characters.availability: Present in early feast, staff-table scenes, Muggle Studies references, and the late collapse of Hogwarts discipline.

characters.physical_details: Thin, middle-aged scholar with reading glasses on a chain and ink-stained fingers from years of manuscript work.

key_relationships.gavin_tench: Student whose manuscript page she identifies as unprecedented and primer-like, then chooses not to assist further.
key_relationships.amycus_carrow: Dangerous reason she keeps strictly inside syllabus boundaries.
key_relationships.alecto_carrow: Part of the regime she is trying not to attract.

characters.occupation_status: Ancient Runes professor, on staff for twenty-three years, not aligned with the Order, surviving this year by caution and professional narrowing.
characters.literacy_languages: English, Latin, Elder Futhark, Ogham, and multiple comparative runic systems; the First Art notation remains beyond her direct fluency.

state_transitions.change: Identifies Gavin's page as unprecedented, probably instructional, and lends Wexworth's comparative notation study.
Hears Gavin's ward-cosmology interpretation, treats it as unverified, and refuses further involvement for safety.

characters.availability: Mostly classroom background; direct continuity relevance in Chapters 4 and 8; survives the battle.

timeline.story_start: September 1, 1997, at the start of the Hogwarts year under Snape's headmastership and the Carrow regime.
timeline.estimated_span: Eight months: September 1, 1997 through dawn on May 2, 1998.

seasonal_track.season: Early autumn to late October 1997: rain, cold corridors, missing students, and the first hidden trips into the seventh-floor sub-collection.
November to mid-December 1997: dark afternoons, worsening occupation, first substrate perception, then the Builders and the failing wards.
Late December 1997: pre-break exhaustion, mock-exam drift, then Christmas Day at skeleton-staff Hogwarts.
January to March 1998: snow, thaw, first successful First Art practice, Duncan's classroom torture, and direct sight of the wards from the Astronomy Tower.
April to late April 1998: daffodils, green grounds, worsening school damage, friendship rupture, and open student resistance.
Night of May 1 into morning of May 2, 1998: Battle of Hogwarts, dawn aftermath, and return to the manuscript.

key_events.event: Start of term under Snape and the Carrows
Gavin notices Bagshot's footnote about a pre-wand magical substrate
Restricted Section attempt, Carrow detention, and discovery of the hidden catalogue room
Gavin removes the fifty-seven-sheet manuscript from the sub-collection
Professor Babbling identifies the notation as unprecedented and lends Wexworth
Gavin first perceives substrate beneath a simple Levitation Charm
Gavin decodes the Builders and then the planetary wards
Gavin confirms the wards are degrading and panics in the sub-collection
Christmas Day reprieve; Gavin feels ward pressure directly for the first time
First successful First Art edit: Gavin loosens the visibility agreement around his school quill
Duncan is tortured in Dark Arts class while Gavin watches without intervening
Astronomy Tower perception of the wards and the condition beyond them
Miren and Gavin's friendship breaks after he refuses to step back from the manuscript
Gavin realizes he could disable the root grammar behind lethal dark magic
Student resistance becomes semi-open; the Room of Requirement starts absorbing students
Battle of Hogwarts and Voldemort's fall
key_events.timing: Night of May 1 to dawn of May 2, 1998

key_events.event: Great Hall triage, Marsh/Anand/Creevey/Lupin/Tonks among the dead, and Miren finally believes Gavin

geography.primary_setting: Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry in the Scottish Highlands, under occupation for almost the entire story.

key_locations.name: Hufflepuff common room and dormitories
key_locations.details: Barrel entrance near the kitchens; round, earthy, warm, low-ceilinged, and heavily scented by bread, onions, and woodsmoke. Becomes the novel's main refuge space and the place where daily damage is most visibly absorbed.

key_locations.name: Kitchens and kitchen corridor
key_locations.details: Warm service space beside the Hufflepuff basement. Its bread heat, pipes, and house-elf traffic make it a survival/logistics spine for food, contraband supplies, and later Great Hall water runs.

key_locations.name: Great Hall
key_locations.details: Assembly and meal space under Snape's chair and the enchanted ceiling. The hall hosts the Chapter 6 Cruciatus lesson, the battle's end, and the post-battle triage ward.

key_locations.name: Hogwarts library and Restricted Section
key_locations.details: Upper-floor research space run by Madam Pince. The Bagshot footnote is found here; the manuscript itself is not. Pince knows more than she admits about missing parts of the collection.

key_locations.name: The sub-collection
key_locations.details: Older seventh-floor stone rooms with self-lit walls, mineral-cold air, and shelves carved directly into limestone. Hidden by shifting corridor routes and not the same thing as the Room of Requirement. This is Gavin's real workspace and the manuscript's original storage site.

key_locations.name: Dark Arts classroom
key_locations.details: Former Defence classroom repurposed into a punishment floor space with desks against the walls, scorch marks, and later bloodstains that remain visible.

key_locations.name: Ancient Runes classroom
key_locations.details: Babbling's small scholarly room, still recognizably academic even under occupation.

key_locations.name: Astronomy Tower
key_locations.details: Highest accessible point of the castle; exposed, windy, and where Gavin finally perceives the ward architecture clearly against the sky.

key_locations.name: Room of Requirement and nearby seventh-floor routes
key_locations.details: Late-novel student refuge run through Neville's network. By Chapter 16 it is swallowing students who stop returning to ordinary dorm life.

distances: Hufflepuff common room to the Great Hall is roughly two minutes on the same lower level.
Hufflepuff common room to the library requires multiple stair flights and is a meaningful hike rather than a quick errand.
The seventh-floor route from ordinary castle corridors into the sub-collection can take anything from about five minutes to half an hour depending on how the castle is connecting itself that night.
Great Hall to Astronomy Tower is one of the castle's longest internal climbs.
The kitchens, Hufflepuff common room, and their adjacent corridors form a practical logistics zone for food, water, and informal movement.

world_rules: The First Art edits substrate agreements beneath wand magic; it is not cast with wandwork or incantations.
The manuscript teaches by restructuring the reader's cognition. Long exposure changes what Gavin can perceive whether he wants it to or not.
The Builders used magic as a medium of existence, not as a tool, and relocated rather than simply dying out.
Planetary wards make Earth effectively unregistered to the condition beyond them; the wards have been drifting out of tune for centuries.
What lies beyond the wards is not a sentient monster but an incompatible condition or environment.
Large-scale magical combat saturates the substrate and makes precise First Art work much harder or temporarily impossible.
Human First Art use costs cognition and body: headaches, nosebleeds, visual distortion, fatigue, and worsening strain with scale.
Major ward repairs are shape-based simultaneous operations; trying to decompose them into sequential human-scale edits destabilizes the thin place further.
Substrate edits on living bodies do not heal the way ordinary magic heals; they remove the injury's validity and would leave the wrong medical trace if completed cleanly.
Cruciatus, Imperius, and the Killing Curse share a deeper substrate permission that lets one caster overrule another body.
Voldemort's fall collapses the Dark Mark network and organized dark-magic bindings, but it does not affect the cosmic ward problem.

power_structure.holder: Voldemort and the Death Eater regime
power_structure.over: Wizarding Britain and, through it, Hogwarts
power_structure.mechanism: Ministry capture, terror, blood-status law, and lethal dark magic until the battle's end.
Formal institutional authority backed by the regime.
Mandatory Dark Arts and Muggle Studies, corridor patrols, detentions, and public torture.

power_structure.holder: Dumbledore's Army / Room of Requirement network
power_structure.over: Student logistics, hiding, and mutual aid
power_structure.mechanism: Supply runs, message drops, healing distribution, safe rooms, and lookout shifts.

power_structure.holder: The manuscript and the First Art
power_structure.over: Gavin's perception, priorities, and ability set
power_structure.mechanism: Cognitive restructuring followed by direct substrate literacy.

objects.name: A History of Magic (Gavin's copy)
objects.origin: Standard Hogwarts history textbook by Bathilda Bagshot.
objects.status: Page 742 contains the footnote that starts the entire plot by mentioning a pre-wand magical substrate manuscript in the Hogwarts collection.

objects.name: The Builders' manuscript
objects.origin: Pre-Foundation teaching instrument stored in the hidden sub-collection.
objects.status: Fifty-seven warm, unbound, moving sheets. Gavin first hides it in his trunk, later carries pages in a satchel or leather folder and stages them on stone shelves/tables while working.

objects.name: Gavin's wand
objects.status: Still needed for routine school magic even as Gavin's real growth moves below wand-level grammar.

objects.name: Wexworth's comparative notation study
objects.origin: A worn green reference text on structural invariants in unknown notation systems.
objects.status: Babbling lends it privately to Gavin; it gives him the only usable first framework for the manuscript.

objects.name: Altered school quill
objects.origin: Cheap school quill used for repeated First Art practice.
objects.status: In Chapter 11 Gavin loosens its visibility agreement. It stays blurry, is poor for writing, and functions as Gavin's crude gauge for local ward strain.

knowledge_state.knows: The manuscript is a teaching device that changes its reader and allows perception of the substrate beneath ordinary magic.
The Builders used magic as a medium of existence and relocated rather than dying.
Earth is protected by thinning planetary wards and the condition beyond them is incompatible with magical life.
He can directly edit substrate agreements; the first proven object edit is the altered quill.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone. Miren only sees the result as a weak Disillusionment-like blur.

knowledge_state.knows: The shared root grammar under Cruciatus, Imperius, and the Killing Curse can be spoiled locally in combat or globally with enough quiet working time.
A mapped ward repair can require seventeen simultaneous parameters; Gavin can consciously hold six or seven, so full maintenance exceeds normal human cognition.
Substrate correction on living tissue would erase an injury's validity instead of leaving ordinary magical-healing traces, which trained healers could notice.
Gavin's manuscript obsession is making him miss food, classes, resistance work, and ordinary social presence.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Nobody close to him; she voices the concern repeatedly.

knowledge_state.knows: Gavin claims the world is hidden by failing wards and threatened by a cosmic condition outside them.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: She keeps it mostly to herself because she cannot verify it and does not believe it then.

knowledge_state.knows: Gavin can locally dissolve lethal or controlling curses and his ward story is true even if she cannot perceive the mechanism.
The manuscript page Gavin showed her is unprecedented, pre-Futhark in feel, and probably instructional.
Gavin is interpreting the manuscript as a ward-cosmology, but she treats the conclusion as unverified and stress-amplified.
He is specifically targeted as a Muggle-born and suspects that going home could expose his parents.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: The home-risk part is mostly kept to himself.

environmental_constants: Hufflepuff spaces smell of earth, warm wood, and kitchens; pipe noise and house-elf work carry through the walls.
Occupied Hogwarts is quieter; students move quickly and learn corridors as risk maps.
The sub-collection is dry, mineral-cold, limestone-smelling, and stone-lit.
Lower floors feel colder than upper castle levels during the Carrow year.
The Astronomy Tower is windy, exposed, and visually indifferent, making it the clearest place for Gavin's cosmic perception.
Seasonal track stays consistent: autumn rain and mud, winter snow and pipe noise, then spring daffodils and thaw.
